Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.

When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The ascii shows the container area. The player (N = light green) is at rows 40-41, cols 12-13. The container is gray (g) and goes from rows 38-43, cols 8-13. The player is at the right edge of the container.
Looking at the pattern:
- Rows 38-39: ccggggggccc (gray block, 6 wide)
- Row 40: ccggggNNccc (gray + player)
- Row 41: ccggggNNccc (gray + player)
- Rows 42-43: ccggggggccc (gray block, 6 wide)
The container is 6 cells wide (cols 8-13) and 6 cells tall (rows 38-43). The player is a 2x2 block at the right edge.
Above the container, there are blue pixels forming a checkerboard pattern at rows 34-37, cols 8-11. And above that, there's a gray block at rows 30-33, cols 8-11.
The player seems to be inside this gray container. Let me try moving UP to see if the player can exit the container. But first, let me check what changed between the last few frames to understand the game mechanics better.
Actually, looking at the images more carefully, the left side has colored blocks and the right side has target shapes. The white dashed line separates them. The player is in the bottom-left area.
Let me try to understand what the goal is. The previous model mentioned clicking on shapes on the right. But now I see the player is a green block inside a gray container. Maybe the player needs to navigate to certain positions or interact with the colored blocks.
Let me try moving the player UP to see if it can exit the container and reach the blue pixel pattern above.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content: The container is 6x6 (rows 38-43, cols 8-13), player is a 2x2 green block at the right edge. Above it are blue checkerboard pixels (rows 34-37) and another gray block (rows 30-33). Let me try moving UP to exit the container.

sections.content:

<function=python>

action(['UP'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
